question: What is the recommended tyre pressure for a mountain bike?
answer: This question does not appear to be covered by the current corpus, so I will not answer it rather than risk inventing details. The most related records found are listed below.

sources.title: K-200 Sonar System Interface Control Document v2.0
sources.body:
DOC-462 | K-200 Sonar System Interface Control Document v2.0

# K-200 Sonar System Interface Control Document

Document number: MD-DOC-462-B. Revision: B. Type: ICD. Author: Cass Doyle. Created: 2023-08-01. Last modified: 2024-10-15. Status: Released.

## Change History
- Rev A: Initial sonar ICD for DepthWave transducer (2023-08-01)
- Rev B: Updated for AquaSound Corp transducer (P-1148 Rev B) per ECN-315 / ECR-218 (2024-10-15)

## §2 Transducer — P-1148 Rev B (AquaSound Corp)

Supplier: AquaSound Corp. Frequency range: 100–400kHz broadband. Centre frequency: 200kHz. Frequency stability: ±1.5kHz over operating temperature (-10°C to +50°C). Depth rating: 30m. Connector: IP68 Subconn circular. DNV-GL certified.

The original DepthWave supplier was replaced after KES-158 (frequency drift bug) documented 16kHz drift over thermal soak. Vendor selection rationale: WIKI-045 (Sonar Vendor Selection Meeting, 2024-08-29). Change authority: ECN-315.

## §6 Sonar Mounting

### §6.1 Mounting Bracket

Sonar transducer P-1148 is mounted on bracket P-1149 (Sonar Mounting Bracket, aluminium 6061-T6). See DRW-517 for full mounting drawing.

### §6.2 Cant Angle — Critical Design Parameter

sources.title: Comms datalink encryption upgrade: AES-256-GCM via P-1127 FPGA, radio P-1125→P-1126
sources.body:
ECR-229 | Comms datalink encryption upgrade: AES-256-GCM via P-1127 FPGA, radio P-1125→P-1126

Engineering Change Request ECR-229.

Raised by: Priya Nair (Comms/Datalink Engineer), 2025-01-29.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Felix Herrera (Systems Integration), 2025-02-05.

Summary: Add AES-256-GCM hardware encryption module (P-1127 FPGA) to the datalink, and upgrade the radio from P-1125 (FreeWave 500kbps) to P-1126 (FreeWave 2Mbps dual-band) to accommodate the bandwidth overhead.

Rationale: A penetration test conducted in December 2024 (report MD-SEC-2024-11) found that the K-200 command and telemetry link was vulnerable to command injection — an attacker with a compatible FreeWave radio could inject spoofed command packets. The customer's security addendum (contract KES-203) requires AES-256-GCM end-to-end encryption on all command and telemetry traffic by programme milestone M4 (March 2025). This was agreed at WIKI-071 (Comms Encryption Upgrade Review, 2025-01-22).

Architecture (from WIKI-071): The P-1127 FPGA module (Lattice iCE40, implementing AES-256-GCM in hardware) sits between the datalink controller (P-1121) and the radio (P-1125/P-1126). Session keys are derived via ECDH on each power cycle, with key rotation every 60 minutes. The AES-256-GCM 16-byte auth tag and protocol overhead add approximately 3% throughput overhead. At 500kbps (P-1125) this is marginal; the P-1126 at 2Mbps provides sufficient headroom for full telemetry at 200ms update rate.

sources.title: K-200 Comms System Interface Control Document v3.0 (Rev C)
sources.body:
DOC-471 | K-200 Comms System Interface Control Document v3.0 (Rev C)

# K-200 Comms System Interface Control Document

Document number: MD-DOC-471-C. Revision: C. Type: ICD. Author: Priya Nair. Created: 2023-06-01. Last modified: 2025-02-28. Status: Released.

## Change History
- Rev A: 900MHz FHSS baseline, P-1125 (2023-06-01)
- Rev B: Diversity antenna system added (2024-03-01)
- Rev C: AES-256-GCM encryption layer added, radio P-1125→P-1126, P-1127 FPGA module (2025-02-28, per ECN-322)

## §3 Radio Subsystem — P-1126 Enhanced

Current radio: FreeWave Enhanced Dual-Band (P-1126 Rev B). Frequency: 900MHz / 2.4GHz FHSS. Data rate: 2Mbps. Output power: 1W. Range: 15km at 1W. The P-1126 replaced P-1125 (500kbps) to accommodate the bandwidth overhead of AES-256-GCM encryption. Change authority: ECN-322.

## §4 Encryption — P-1127 FPGA Module

All command and telemetry traffic is encrypted with AES-256-GCM before transmission by the P-1127 FPGA encryption module (Lattice iCE40, hardware implementation). The FPGA module sits between the datalink controller (P-1121) and the radio (P-1126). Session keys are derived via ECDH on power-cycle, with 60-minute rotation. Implementation: SRC-018 (encryption_handler.c). GCS protocol stack: SRC-024 (gcs_ui_backend.py).

Security requirement source: Customer contract security addendum, KES-203, penetration test MD-SEC-2024-11. Design review: WIKI-071.

## §5 Protocol — see DOC-472

Detailed protocol specification in DOC-472 (Comms Protocol Specification Rev B).
